channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: America’s largest banks are building a new digital currency network to stop a massive deposit drain
item.description: America’s biggest banks are launching tokenized deposits to compete with stablecoins, opening a new front in the race to become the dominant form of cash on blockchain networks....

item.title: May jobs report explained: Why 172,000 jobs means higher rates, pricier loans, and a Bitcoin drop
item.description: The US economy added 172,000 jobs in May, more than double the 80,000 that Wall Street economists had expected, and the unemployment rate held at 4.3%. The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) also revise...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description:
OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks.
The feature is primarily desi...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
item.description:
It got stupid again.
The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is normal. F...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
item.description:
A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa.
These efforts have been complement...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
item.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Error 524 Decoy: Desenmascarando una Operación Global de Smishing Oculta Tras Páginas de Error
item.description: Los investigadores de Group-IB exponen una operación de smishing y phishing a gran escala que suplanta más de 260 marcas en 72 países, utilizando páginas de error 524 falsas para evadir el análisis....
item.dc:creator: GroupIB Blog

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrenc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
item.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
item.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
item.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
item.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
item.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
item.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Fixes One-Click GitHub Dev Attack That Let Attackers Steal OAuth Tokens
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token.
"Just by clicking a link, it's possible f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
item.description: Redis has patched a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. The flaw was found by an autonomous AI t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
